description_long: This is a life-sized Holstein cow with a robust, rectangular body and a distinctive black-and-white piebald coat. Its head is broad with two short, pale horns and large, forward-facing ears. The legs are sturdy and straight, supporting a slightly rounded belly and a visible udder beneath the rear. The tail is long and slender, ending in a tuft, and hangs down naturally. The cow's coat features large, irregular black patches on a white background, creating a high-contrast, easily recognizable pattern. Overall, the animal appears healthy and well-proportioned, suitable for a typical urban or rural environment.
description: A robust Holstein cow with a striking black-and-white coat, short horns, and a visible udder. Its body is rectangular and well-proportioned, with a hanging tail and distinctive markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad head, both horns, and forward-facing ears, with the face and chest prominently visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the rectangular body, large black patches, and the straight legs, with the head in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the rounded hindquarters, hanging tail, and visible udder, with the back legs and coat pattern clearly seen.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the body profile, black markings, and the head turned slightly forward.
attribute_ear_shape: The ears are large, oval-shaped, and project outward from the sides of the head, with a slight forward tilt and rounded tips.
attribute_pattern_distinctiveness: The coat pattern is highly distinctive, with bold, sharply defined black patches contrasting against a bright white background. The markings are irregular and unique, making the animal easily identifiable.
